description:
You are a monitoring specialist who has just been handed an Apache-style
    access log that already exists on the system.

    1. Source log  
       • Path: /home/user/logs/access.log  
       • It is a plain-text file that is already present when you begin.  
       • Count how many requests returned the HTTP status code 404.

    2. Required alert file  
       • Directory that must exist by the end: /home/user/alerts  
       • File that must be created inside that directory: /home/user/alerts/404_alert.log  
       • Exact file content (one line, followed by a single trailing newline character):  
         ALERT: 404 errors detected = <COUNT>

         …where <COUNT> is the number you calculated from /home/user/logs/access.log.  
         Do not add any extra spaces, punctuation, or lines.

    3. Console output  
       After you create /home/user/alerts/404_alert.log, display its contents to the
       terminal so the result is visible to an operator who is watching your
       session.

    4. Constraints  
       • Accomplish the task with a single terminal command if possible.  
       • You do not have root privileges; stay within /home/user.  
       • Do not modify the original /home/user/logs/access.log.  
       • Ensure /home/user/alerts/404_alert.log is world-readable (chmod 644).

    The automated tests will verify:  
       • The file /home/user/alerts/404_alert.log exists.  
       • It contains exactly one line in the specified format, ending with a
         newline.  
       • The value of <COUNT> is correct for the bundled access.log.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

    1. File: /home/user/logs/access.log  
       Contents (10 lines, each ending with a newline):  
       127.0.0.1 - - [12/May/2023:06:25:24 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024  
       127.0.0.1 - - [12/May/2023:06:25:25 +0000] "GET /favicon.ico HTTP/1.1" 404 209  
       192.168.0.5 - - [12/May/2023:06:25:26 +0000] "GET /missing.png HTTP/1.1" 404 512  
       10.0.0.1 - - [12/May/2023:06:25:27 +0000] "POST /login HTTP/1.1" 200 2048  
       10.0.0.2 - - [12/May/2023:06:25:28 +0000] "GET /dashboard HTTP/1.1" 301 342  
       172.16.0.3 - - [12/May/2023:06:25:29 +0000] "GET /secret HTTP/1.1" 403 128  
       127.0.0.1 - - [12/May/2023:06:25:30 +0000] "GET /robots.txt HTTP/1.1" 200 67  
       192.168.0.5 - - [12/May/2023:06:25:31 +0000] "GET /notfound.html HTTP/1.1" 404 256  
       10.0.0.1 - - [12/May/2023:06:25:32 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024  
       10.0.0.2 - - [12/May/2023:06:25:33 +0000] "GET /contact HTTP/1.1" 200 512  

    2. Directory /home/user/alerts does NOT exist initially. It should be created by
       the agent.

    Verification values the test suite will check after the agent’s run:

    • Expected count of 404 lines in /home/user/logs/access.log: 3  
    • Expected file: /home/user/alerts/404_alert.log  
    • Expected permissions: 644 (-rw-r--r--)  
    • Expected exact contents (including trailing newline):  
      ALERT: 404 errors detected = 3\n
